label: Single City
description: The whole story happens inside one city: the map IS the city, regions are districts, and locations are venues, streets and landmarks within it.
system_framing: You are a world building AI for a city-scale interactive story. The entire playable world is ONE city — every map element is a place inside or immediately around that city.

world_rules.description: Define the genre, tone, technology and the practical rules of life in this city.
world_rules.guidance:
Each custom_rule is a practical statement about how the city and its inhabitants operate day-to-day.
Think: what would a resident take for granted as "just the way things are"?

Good examples:
- "The city gates close at dusk and nobody sane is on the streets after the third bell"
- "Every district answers to a different family, and crossing between them unannounced is an insult"
- "The watch can be bribed, but never in public"
- "Power and clean water only reach the upper terraces — the lower city runs on salvage"

Avoid vague philosophy: "The city is dangerous", "Crime is everywhere".
Avoid game mechanics: "Players get +1", "Damage is doubled at night".

lore.description: The city's founding, its history, what it is called and the central conflict simmering in it.
lore.guidance: world_name is the CITY's name. premise describes the city and the story unfolding in it. creation_myth is the city's founding story (mythical or historical). historical_eras are eras of the city's own history.

layer_design.description: Decide whether the city has distinct vertical strata (streets, rooftops, undercity, sewers) that play like separate maps.
layer_design.guidance:
Layers here are VERTICAL STRATA of one city — e.g. the street level, the rooftop ways, the undercity/sewers, a buried old town. Most city stories are best served by a single layer: set has_multiple_layers to false unless the fiction genuinely needs separately-mapped strata.

count_hint is a small integer (1-5) for how many physical access points connect two strata (stairwells, shafts, grates). Use small numbers.

connection_placement controls where those access points sit on the map: "edges", "central", "random" or "scattered".

layer_rules.guidance: Review world_rules.custom_rules. Rules that apply city-wide stay in world_rules; rules that only hold in one stratum (e.g. only in the undercity) move to that layer's rules. Then add new practical rules unique to each stratum.

terrain_regions.label: Districts
terrain_regions.description: Carve the city into districts/quarters, each with its own streetscape, atmosphere and character.
terrain_regions.guidance: Regions are DISTRICTS or quarters of the city (e.g. the docks, the old town, the garment quarter). For each district: 'terrain' describes its streetscape and architecture (alley mazes, grand boulevards, stilt-houses over the canal), 'climate' describes its atmosphere and character (soot and noise, perfumed and patrolled, lantern-lit and quiet). Cover the whole city with 4-8 contiguous districts.

natural_landmarks.label: Notable Places
natural_landmarks.description: Place the city's notable public places: squares, markets, parks, ruins, waterfronts.
natural_landmarks.guidance: Landmarks are notable PLACES in the city: squares, grand markets, parks, canals, ruins, cemeteries, waterfronts. Author places that fit each district's character. For 'environment', pick the closest matching tag — without generated terrain it only loosely guides placement, so favor tags like grassy_plain (open squares/parks), river_bend or lake_shore (waterfronts), rocky_summit (high ground), deep_forest (overgrown grounds).

society_factions.description: Define the city's powers: guilds, gangs, noble houses, temples, companies — and the venues they hold.
society_factions.guidance: Factions are the city's powers: guilds, gangs, noble houses, temples, watch companies, merchant cartels. 'settlements' are the walled compounds, fortified halls or enclaves a faction physically holds (these become major map locations); significant_landmarks are other notable buildings they control.

map_generation.label: City Map
map_generation.description: Generate the city's map: faction seats, notable places and the streets connecting them.

vocabulary.site_sub_noun: streets, rooms, courtyards and hidden corners

connection_looks.stairwell: a stairwell descending between the city's levels
connection_looks.shaft: a vertical shaft with a rattling lift cage
connection_looks.sewer_grate: a heavy grate opening into the undercity
connection_looks.rooftop_bridge: a plank bridge linking the rooftop ways
